extra.reasoning:
The property requires rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true.

rec_drug_a can only be set true by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires ONLY when the full 8-way conjunction holds: g6pd_deficiency AND seizure_history AND hypertension AND hepatic_impairment AND elderly AND hyperkalemia AND pregnancy AND ckd_dialysis. Otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. So rec_drug_a = true REQUIRES that exact conjunction.

rec_drug_b is governed by two rules: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF g6pd_deficiency THEN rec_drug_b := true) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, IF the same 8-way conjunction THEN rec_drug_b := false).

Critical interaction: the only scenario where rec_drug_a is true is when the 8-way conjunction holds. But that same conjunction is exactly the antecedent of guard_suppress_b, which has priority 90 — higher than rec_b_from_trigger (50). In that scenario both b-rules match (g6pd is part of the conjunction so the trigger fires too), but the higher-priority guard wins, forcing rec_drug_b := false. Therefore whenever rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b = false.

Conversely, when rec_drug_b = true (g6pd_deficiency true but the full conjunction not satisfied, so the guard does not fire), rec_drug_a is false because its rule needs the complete conjunction. When the conjunction is false, rec_drug_a defaults false regardless.

The guard's priority (90 > 50) precisely covers the unique input region that enables rec_drug_a, so (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) is unsatisfiable. The property holds for the entire input space.
